scenario: Helios Telco operates a Keycloak realm for 15000 users that authenticates a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot worker fleet. The deployment manifest still uses the legacy common.keycloak.* prefix and must migrate the token-url key to the documented replacement listed in the properties reference before the next release window.
question: Which property listed in the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot starter reference replaces the legacy common.keycloak.token-url key?

options.text: The documented replacement property for the legacy common.keycloak.token-url key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.auth.url under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.
The documented replacement property for the legacy common.keycloak.token-url key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.keycloak.token-url under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.
The documented replacement property for the legacy common.keycloak.token-url key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.auth.token-url under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.
The documented replacement property for the legacy common.keycloak.token-url key in Camunda 8.8 is camunda.client.auth.issuer-url under the Spring Boot starter properties reference.

a.text: Incorrect. The 8.8 properties reference uses camunda.client.auth.token-url for token endpoints; there is no camunda.client.auth.url entry in the table.

b.text: Incorrect. The reference removes the keycloak segment entirely; OAuth token endpoints live under camunda.client.auth, not under a camunda.client.keycloak branch.

c.text: Correct. The reference maps both common.keycloak.token-url and common.keycloak.url to the unified camunda.client.auth.token-url key in the 8.8 Spring Boot starter.

d.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.auth.issuer-url is not the documented replacement for token endpoint keys; token URLs migrate to camunda.client.auth.token-url in 8.8.

explanation: In 8.8 the Spring Boot starter folds the legacy common.keycloak.* OAuth keys into camunda.client.auth.*; the documented replacement for common.keycloak.token-url is camunda.client.auth.token-url.
